title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN Cleveland's Andrew Goldhammer claims the Cavaliers prefer the fourth seed to avoid facing the Celtics in the second playoff round.
The Cavaliers have not advanced past the second round since 2018 and faced a notable exit against the Celtics in 2024.
The Celtics have won 10 of their last 12 games, including a season sweep over Cleveland, enhancing playoff confidence.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents face varying electricity costs depending on their location, with municipal utilities generally charging less than investor-owned companies.
Municipal utilities serve 52 communities, offering cheaper rates due to lower maintenance costs and long-term nuclear contracts.
Switching to municipal utilities or using aggregation can decrease costs, but infrastructure purchases can be expensive.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students rally for better communication from administration after violent incidents near campus.
A fatal police shooting near campus heightened safety concerns among students who rely on external news sources.
University alerts were criticized as inadequate despite recent violent events occurring near Northeastern's campus.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University President Melissa Gilliam paused a signage policy after protests over removed Pride flags from campus.
The policy prohibited outward-facing materials on university property, leading to the removal of Pride flags in March.
A coalition of students, faculty, and alumni petitioned for policy reevaluation, emphasizing support for LGBTQ+ community members.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Red Sox trade right-handed pitcher Patrick Galle to Cardinals, completing Sonny Gray deal.
Galle was drafted by Boston in the 17th round from Ole Miss, turned 22 recently.
Sonny Gray, 36, has a 4.50 ERA this season; scheduled to start against Brewers Tuesday.

title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Episode 2 recap
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Liz McGraw resists being called 'Grandma' by pregnant step-daughter Brianna, preferring 'Gigi' at family meeting.
Rulla Nehme Pontarelli discusses being excluded due to rumors of her husband's alleged affair starting in Portsmouth.
Jo-Ellen Tiberi faces blame from Rulla for spreading cheating rumors and reacts aggressively to attempts affecting her livelihood.

title: Mass. House to vote on youth social media ban, strict school cellphone ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts House is set to vote on a bill banning social media for children under 14 and requiring parental consent for ages 14-15.
The proposed legislation includes a 'bell-to-bell' cellphone ban in schools, requiring state guidance and a pilot program in 10 districts.
Quincy is implementing a school cellphone ban with secure pouches for students, aiming to reduce distractions and improve classroom focus.

title: Maine father dies after saving his children from a rip current
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, from North Yarmouth, Maine, died saving his children from a rip current in Juno Beach, Florida.
Palm Beach County Fire Department conducted a rescue after the incident; Jennings' children survived, but he did not.
A GoFundMe for Jennings' family has raised over $170,000 following his death on April 1 in an unguarded swimming area.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon to support neglected and at-risk kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sean Meehan, 28, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support The Home For Little Wanderers.
The Home For Little Wanderers offers care and services for at-risk children facing abuse and neglect.
Meehan's inspiration to run came after witnessing his sister complete the Boston Marathon in 2023.

title: The Boston Marathon didn’t make this list of best marathons in America. Do you think it should have?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: USA Today did not include the Boston Marathon in its 2026 10Best Readers' Choice list of top US marathons.
The Boston Marathon, scheduled for April 20, attracts 30,000 runners and 500,000 spectators annually.
The marathon is the world’s oldest, features iconic landmarks, and is among the Abbott World Marathon Majors.
